title: Recover from a stale CMake cache
description: A procedure to identify and clear cached variables that no longer reflect the intended configuration.
body: Use when configure picks up old compiler paths or option values that no longer apply. Do not edit CMakeCache.txt by hand to patch around it. Steps: inspect cached values with cmake -LAH; if a fundamental setting like the compiler changed, delete the build directory and reconfigure clean; otherwise override the specific cache variable with -D. Validation signals include a configure that reflects the current toolchain and a clean rebuild. Risk boundary: a stale cache can silently keep building with the wrong compiler, so prefer a clean build directory when core settings change.
